role: Settings.tsx is the full-viewport consolidated settings modal surface that hosts the Brain, Modes, Voice, Roles, Composition, and Cognition configuration sections.
capabilities: full-viewport modal settings shell with left section rail and close handling
Brain & persona: model picker with load-on-pick, temperature, max-tokens, context-window set-and-restart, endpoint, timeout, persona switching, and resource-fit bar
Modes: hierarchical mode registry display, live mode switching, and off/suggest/auto auto-detect toggle
Voice: voice on/off, listening style, STT ear selection, ear status + start service, TTS engine selection, voice argument, per-engine knob display, and voice path selection
Model roles: role registry display and per-role model binding
Composition: read-only R2 context-ranking knob display
Cognition: direct role creation, role execution, run-index display, and run refresh
global settings busy and error surfacing
ui-ref targets for settings close, brain, modes, voice, roles, composition, cognition, role cards, and run addresses
substance_why: It implements a six-section modal with wired interactions for model loading, persona switching, mode switching, voice engine/path selection, role model binding, and cognition role create/run/run-index, plus a resource-fit bar and error/loading states.
interactions_present: close modal by scrim click or close button
switch settings section via left rail
select a chat model to load on pick
set temperature and max output tokens on blur
set context window and restart local service
set endpoint base URL and reply timeout on blur
switch persona by clicking a persona card
switch mode by clicking a mode card
toggle mode auto-detect via pill buttons
toggle voice enabled via select
set listening style via select
select STT ear via select
start a down ear service via button
select/clear TTS engine by clicking engine card
set voice argument override on blur
select voice path by clicking path card
bind a model to a role via select
create a cognition role via form + button
run a cognition role via form + button
refresh cognition runs via button
interactions_missing: Composition knobs are read-only with no live setter
per-engine TTS knobs are displayed but not adjustable
cognition lacks output-schema editor, richer input wiring (run://, context://, vec://), per-role model binding, role edit/delete, and rule attach/detach
no control to refresh the resource-fit report
context-window input is not prefilled from current config
no search/filter across personas, models, modes, engines, paths, roles, or runs
no keyboard shortcut (e.g. Esc) to close the modal
no inline form validation before backend call
no s2s working UI (only noted as unavailable)
performance_risks: the entire modal and all local form state live in one component, so any keystroke re-renders the whole settings surface
no memoization of derived lists (modes, engines, paths, roles, runs, personas) or repeated .find/.findIndex calls
no virtualization for potentially long persona/mode/engine/path/role/run lists
blur-triggered API writes for numeric/text fields fire immediately without debounce
reuse_why: The consolidated settings surface and its section breakdown are the right architecture, but the file is an overgrown monolith with honest gaps (read-only composition, display-only engine knobs, limited cognition authoring) that the redesign should split and complete.
decomposition_hints: split the settings shell (modal scrim, rail, masthead, error/busy) from section content
move each section (Brain, Modes, Voice, Roles, Composition, Cognition) into its own module
split CognitionSection into RoleAuthor, RoleRunner, and RunIndex sub-components
extract reusable form primitives (SetField, SetSelect, SetInput, SetPill) from duplicated markup
co-locate derived registry projections and memoization with their sections or in dedicated selectors
isolate the read-only Composition section until live setters are designed
